title: What Resistance to Tinnitus Reveals About Resistance to Life
phrases: The body remembers what the mind would prefer to file away.
You cannot think your way into a felt sense of safety. The body has its own logic.
The gap between stimulus and response is where your entire life lives.
We are not our thoughts, but we are responsible for our relationship to them.

title: How Suffering Transforms When You Stop Running from Sound
phrases: The algorithm of your attention determines the landscape of your experience.
The question is never whether the pain will come. The question is whether you'll meet it with presence or with narrative.

title: The Difference Between Pain and Suffering in Tinnitus
phrases: Consciousness doesn't arrive. It's what's left when everything else quiets down.
Every moment of genuine attention is a small act of liberation.
What we call 'the present moment' is not a place you go. It's the only place you've ever been.

The wellness industry sells solutions to problems it helps you believe you have.

title: How Tinnitus Teaches Radical Acceptance
phrases: We are not our thoughts, but we are responsible for our relationship to them.

title: The Observer and the Observed in Tinnitus Experience
phrases: The most sophisticated defense mechanism is the one that looks like wisdom.

title: How Awareness Changes Your Relationship with Ringing
phrases: The gap between stimulus and response is where your entire life lives.

title: Meditation Practices Designed for People with Tinnitus
phrases: Embodiment is not a technique. It's what happens when you stop living exclusively in your head.

title: How to Use Tinnitus as Your Meditation Object
phrases: The self you're trying to improve is the same self doing the improving. Notice the circularity.

title: The Breath as Anchor When Sound Cannot Be Escaped
phrases: Freedom is not the absence of constraint. It's the capacity to choose your relationship to it.

title: Walking Meditation for Tinnitus-Related Restlessness
phrases: Most people don't fear change. They fear the gap between who they were and who they haven't become yet.
